data.title: Stock index futures muted day after S&P 500, Nasdaq scale record closing highs
data.description:
Stock index futures were little changed on Thursday, a day after the S&P 500 and Nasdaq touched record highs.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) were unchanged, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.1%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3 basis points to 4.21%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.16%.

Major equity averages ended at record highs on Wednesday, fronted by climbing shares for Salesforce (CRM) and other tech companies, while Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell made upbeat remarks about the world's largest economy.

"Jerome Powell signaled that the Federal Reserve would react to rather than pre-empt any consumer taxes imposed by U.S. President-elect Donald Trump via tariffs. That should allow U.S. interest rates to continue to decline in the first half of next year," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

"It’s been a very eventful 24 hours for markets, with the French government losing a no-confidence vote for the first time since 1962, alongside ongoing political uncertainty in South Korea and some hawkish-leaning comments from Powell," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"But despite everything that’s happening, risk assets have been broadly unphased by the various developments," Allen added.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) also crossed the 100K milestone on Wednesday.

On the economic side, U.S. employers' announced job cuts rose 3.8% in November, climbed 27% Y/Y, while the initial jobless claims increased more than expected in past week. The two labor data came just a day ahead of the November nonfarm payrolls report.

The Fed balance sheet is due later in the day.

data.title: Oracle in focus as Guggenheim, Jefferies up price targets ahead of Q2 results
data.description:
Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) was in focus on Thursday as research firms Guggenheim and Jefferies upped their price target on the IT giant ahead of its fiscal second-quarter results, set to be released after the close of trading on December 9.

Shares rose 0.6% in premarket trading.

"We expect Oracle to at least meet F2Q25 expectations and guide F3Q25 in line," Guggenheim analyst John DiFucci wrote in a note to clients. "Our field checks indicated solid F2Q25 momentum in Oracle’s Cloud business as the company leverages its unique technology advantages across SaaS and IaaS, while pipelines for the second half of the FY are highly optimistic."

DiFucci continued: "ORCL’s AI strategy of embedding incremental services within its core platform is also becoming a meaningful differentiator and is accelerating the pace of on-premise application migrations to SaaS where customers can realize those benefits."

DiFucci, who reiterated his Buy rating and upped his price target to $220 from $200, added there has been more traction with Oracle Database via partnerships with hyperscalers, including several deals with Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) Cloud and another one coming with Amazon (AMZN) Web Services, according to a partner. This is notable, given there has not been much out of the Microsoft (MSFT) Azure partnership that went live about a year ago.

"Consensus revenue estimates generally imply reasonable New ARR growth, though IaaS requires New ARR to double, which was the case last quarter (and Oracle did better than that) but we have trust in management’s visibility," DiFucci added.

Jefferies analyst Brent Thill also upped his price target to $220 (from $190), as he noted recent checks suggest "modest pipeline improvement."

"[Fiscal second-quarter] expectations are elevated coming out of a bullish Sept analyst day and LT rev targets that imply a multi-year growth [acceleration]," Thill, who has a Buy rating on Oracle, wrote in a note to clients. "Continued backlog momentum & better backlog-to-[revenue] conversion are needed to keep the stock on its run."

A consensus of analysts expect Oracle to earn $1.48 per share on $14.12B in revenue.

data.title: CACI awarded $290M contract to provide AI and geospatial expertise to NGA
data.description:
• CACI International (CACI) on Thursday said it is an awardee of a five-year contract valued at up to $290 million to provide artificial intelligence and geospatial expertise to the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) under the Luno-A multi-award indefinite delivery, indefinite quantity vehicle.
  • Under this contract, CACI will provide NGA with complete end-to-end geospatial products generated with CACI-built AI solutions.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CNX Resources expands Pennsylvania holdings with $505M bolt-on deal
data.description:
CNX Resources (NYSE:CNX) said Thursday it agreed to acquire the natural gas upstream and associated midstream business of Apex Energy II in the Appalachian Basin for $505M in cash, expanding its holdings in the Marcellus and Utica shale plays.

The acquired assets bring expected 2025 average daily production of 180M-190M cfe/day and EBITDA of $150M-$160M at recent strip prices, the company said.

CNX (CNX) said it expects the deal will be immediately accretive to free cash flow per share.

The transaction "underscores our confidence in the stacked pay development opportunities that have been unlocked from pioneering the deep Utica in this region," President and CEO Nick Deiuliis said.

data.title: Semler Scientific buys 303 additional bitcoins between Nov. 25-Dec. 04
data.description:
• Semler Scientific (NASDAQ:SMLR) acquired 303 bitcoins for $29.3M between Nov. 25 and Dec. 04, 2024 using proceeds generated from its at-the-market offering.
  • The bitcoins were purchased at an average price of $96,779 per bitcoin, inclusive of fees and expenses. As of Dec. 4, the company held 1,873 bitcoins, which were acquired for an aggregate $147.1M at an average purchase price of $78,553 per bitcoin.
  • The company also reported an BTC Yield of 54.7% for the period between Oct. 01-Dec 04. From July 01 (the quarter after Semler Scientific adopted its bitcoin treasury strategy) to Dec 04., Semler Scientific's BTC Yield was 78.7%.

data.title: Bank of Montreal Q4 earnings miss consensus as provision for credit losses rises
data.description:
Bank of Montreal (NYSE:BMO) stock slipped 2.9% in Thursday premarket trading after the Canadian bank's fiscal Q4 earnings trailed the consensus estimate as its provision for credit losses continued to climb. The company also announced plans to buy back up to 20M of its common shares and to increase its dividend.

"Our overall results [for FY2024] were impacted by elevated provisions for credit losses, and we expect quarterly provisions to moderate through 2025 as the business environment improves," said CEO Darryl White. For the year, "BMO delivered good pre-provision pre-tax earnings growth across all operating groups, and we met our commitment to positive operating leverage in each of the last three quarters and for the full year."

Fiscal Q4 adjusted EPS of C$1.90 (US$1.35), missing the consensus of C$2.39, fell from C$2.64 in Q3 and C$2.93 in last year's Q4.

Adjusted revenue for the quarter ended Oct. 31, 2024, was unchanged Q/Q at C$8.37B (US$5.95B) and increased 2% Y/Y.

BMO's (NYSE:BMO) Q4 adjusted net interest income of C$4.85 increased from C$4.81B in the previous quarter and declined from C$4.96B a year ago. Adjusted net interest margin of 1.90% rose from 1.83% in Q3 and was flat with 1.90% in Q4 2023.

Its provision for credit losses jumped to C$1.52B, including C$416M on performing loans, from C$906M in the prior quarter and C$446M a year ago.

Gross impaired loans ratio of 0.86% vs. 0.89% in Q3.

Adjusted noninterest expenses of C$4.88B climbed from C$4.70B in the prior quarter and dropped from C$4.98B a year ago.

Average gross loans and acceptances of C$671.3B rose from C$669.3B in Q3. Average customer deposits of C$702.9B increased from C$688.3B in the previous quarter.

Canadian Personal & Commercial Banking adjusted net income of C$750M fell 18% Q/Q and Y/Y.

U.S. Personal & Commercial Banking adjusted net income of US$242M (C$326M) decreased 39% Q/Q and 51% Y/Y.

BMO Capital Markets adjusted net income of C$270M dropped 31% Q/Q and 43% Y/Y. Global Markets revenue fell 1% Y/Y to C$938B. Investment and Corporate Banking revenue of C$662M declined 1% Q/Q and 6% Y/Y.

BMO Wealth Management adjusted net income of C$328M slid 10% Q/Q and 7% Y/Y.

Earlier, BMO Financial non-GAAP EPS of C$1.90, revenue of C$8.96B

data.title: Toast, Uber announce expanded integration
data.description:
Toast (NYSE:TOST) and Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) have entered an expanded partnership to help restaurants reduce costs and reach more diners with commission-free delivery.

The companies have been partnering since 2021 to deliver orders made through the Uber Eats app.

The expanded pact introduces a new integration between Toast Delivery Services and Uber Direct, enabling restaurants across the U.S. to save on delivery fees, expand their delivery radius, and leverage Uber's delivery network to offer local delivery of orders made by phone and through Toast digital ordering channels.

In addition to providing restaurants with more choice, this expanded integration with Toast will enable restaurants to access commission-free delivery for a low flat fee per order.

Uber Direct is now available on Toast Delivery Services for customers to fulfill deliveries of orders made by phone and through Toast digital ordering channels.

data.title: Southwest Airlines raises Q4 revenue per unit on encouraging travel trends
data.description:
Shares of Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) are moving higher in Thursday’s premarket action after the carrier raised its unit revenue per available seat mile for the current quarter, thanks to strong travel trends and effective efficiency measures.

“[Southwest] is encouraged by recent revenue trends and forward bookings, including fourth quarter holiday travel, and currently expects strong revenue trends and tactical initiative performance to carry into 2025,” the company said in a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Unit revenue is now expected to increase by 5.5% to 7% from initial estimates of up 3.5% to up 5.5%. This corresponds to a ~4% decline in capacity on a year-over-year basis.

On Monday, Southwest (LUV) said it delivered a “stellar” Thanksgiving period performance (Nov 25 to Dec 1), with nearly 27K flights flow with 3.5M passengers. And after the Christmas meltdown of 2022, the carrier was proud to announce that it took the top spot among domestic carriers for on-time performance and had the fewest delays of 45 minutes or more.

According to the Transportation Security Administration, this Thanksgiving weekend set another record with more than 3 million people traveling home on Sunday, breaking the previous holiday record by ~74,000.

Southwest (LUV) shares are up 2.8% and set to open at the highest level in 9 months.
